title: Duty to inform workers of right to join a union: Options assessment - RPC opinion (green-rated)
body: "As part of a broad set of reforms, the Employment Rights Bill proposes a new duty on employers to give a written statement to their workers informing them of their right to join a trade union at the start of their employment and at other times.  On 21 November 2025, the government published an options assessment to support the consultation on how this new duty could be implemented effectively, while minimising the burden on employers.  The Regulatory Policy Committee has rated the options assessment as ‘fit for purpose’. This is based on its scrutiny of the evidence and analysis supporting the case for why regulation is considered to be necessary. "
